XBE Image Builder Tool (ImageBld)

The XBE Image Builder Tool converts PE-formatted executables to the XBE format used by Xbox. It may also be used to report information about an XBE executable.

USAGE: imagebld [switches] [infile]
SwitchDescription
/DEFAULTSAVEIMAGE:imagefile Default save image (XPR format) for this image. For more information, see /DEFAULTSAVEIMAGE in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/DONTMODIFYHD Disable hard disk writing; T: and U: drives are inaccessible. This switch is useful for demo discs and utility titles that should not leave anything on the hard disk. The /DONTMODIFYHD switch is not appropriate for game titles. For more information, see /DONTMODIFYHD in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/DONTMOUNTUD Initialize without mounting a utility drive. For more information, see /DONTMOUNTUD in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/DUMP Print information about an exisiting XBE file, especially in relation to certification requirements.
/FORMATUD Always format the utility drive mounted during initialization. Incompatible with /DONTMOUNTUD. For more information, see /FORMATUD in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/IN:infile Input PE file to process (*.exe). Alternatively, you may specify the input file on the command line following the switches. Either way, you must specify an input file for ImageBld to run.
/INITFLAGS:number Initialization flags (default=0x00000001). You may specify more than one flag at a time by adding their hexadecimal values together.
  • 0x00000001: Mount the utility drive on startup. Omitting this flag is equivalent to specifying the /DONTMOUNTUD switch.
  • 0x00000002: Format the utility drive after mounting. Requires the 0x00000001 flag. Equivalent to specifying the /FORMATUD switch.
  • 0x00000004: Limit a development console to 64 MB of addressable RAM. Equivalent to specifying the /LIMITMEM switch.
  • 0x00000010: Disable hard disk writing. Equivalent to specifying the /DONTMODIFYHD switch.
/INSERTFILE:file,section,[R][N] * Insert file as section. R is read-only. N is no pre-load. For more information, see /INSERTFILE in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/LIMITMEM Limit run-time memory to 64 MB.
/NOLIBWARN Disable unapproved library warning messages. For more information, see /NOLIBWARN in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/NOPRELOAD:section * Don't automatically load this section. For more information, see /NOPRELOAD in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/OUT:outfile Output XE image file (*.xbe). This switch is required for ImageBld to run. For more information, see /OUT in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/STACK:size Set size of stack. For more information, see /STACK in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTALTID:number * Test alt ID for access by alternate images. For more information, see /TESTALTID in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTID:number Test title ID for this image (hexadecimal or decimal number; default is 0x00000000). For more information, see /TESTID in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTLANKEY:key Test LAN key for this image. For more information, see /TESTLANKEY in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTMEDIATYPES:number Test allowed media types for this image. Can be set to the following values.
ConstantValue
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_HARD_DISK 0x00000001
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_X2 0x00000002
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_CD 0x00000004
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_CD 0x00000008
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_5_RO 0x00000010
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_9_RO 0x00000020
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_5_RW 0x00000040
XBEIMAGE_MEDIA_TYPE_DVD_9_RW 0x00000080
For more information, see Preparing for XBox Submission and /TESTMEDIATYPES in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTNAME:titlename Test title name for this image.
/TESTRATINGS:number Test ratings for this image. Can be any of the following values:
ConstantValue
XC_PC_ESRB_ALL 0x00000000
XC_PC_ESRB_ADULT 0x00000001
XC_PC_ESRB_MATURE 0x00000002
XC_PC_ESRB_TEEN 0x00000003
XC_PC_ESRB_EVERYONE 0x00000004
XC_PC_ESRB_KIDS_TO_ADULTS 0x00000005
XC_PC_ESRB_EARLY_CHILDHOOD 0x00000006
For more information, see /TESTRATINGS in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTREGION:number Test regions for this image.
ConstantValue
XC_GAME_REGION_NA 0x00000001
XC_GAME_REGION_JAPAN 0x00000002
XC_GAME_REGION_RESTOFWORLD 0x00000004
For more information, see /TESTREGION in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTSERVICE:number Test online network that this image should connect to (hexadecimal number; default is 0x54524150, representing PartnerNet). For more information, see /TESTSERVICE in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTSIGNKEY:key Test signature key for this image. For more information, see /TESTSIGNKEY in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTVERSION Test version number for this image. For more information, see /TESTVERSION in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TESTXBEVERSION Test XBE version number for this image. This switch differs from /TESTVERSION in that /TESTXBEVERSION only looks at the high 24 bits of the version field in the XBE certificate. The high 24 bits are a title-designated XBE version, and the low 8 bits are a disc version assigned by certification. It is recommended that you use the /TESTXBEVERSION switch instead of /TESTVERSION. For more information, see /TESTXBEVERSION in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/TITLEIMAGE:imagefile Title XPR image file for this image.
/TITLEINFO:infofile Title info file for this image.
/UDCLUSTER:number Utility drive cluster size (default is 16384). May be specified either in kilobytes (16, 32, and 64 are valid values) or in bytes (16384, 32768, and 65536 are valid values). For more information, see /UDCLUSTER in the Visual Studio Property Pages.
/DEBUG Adds information to the .xbe file that enables the Kernel Debugger and WinDBG to find the debug information database. Using this switch does not significantly increase the size of the .xbe file. Also, it does not cause a performance impact unless the Kernel Debugger or WinDBG are actually used. As a result, its use is highly recommended, especially for debug builds.
/? Display help message.

* This switch allows multiple parameters to be specified.

Note   The default value for the /TESTID switch is 0x00000000. If you build your XBE using the Microsoft® Visual Studio® IDE and you do not specify a title ID in the Title Info section of the Xbox Image tab in the Project Settings dialog, no /TESTID is specified; therefore, the default /TESTID for Xbox Game projects is 0x00000000.

The /TITLEINFO File

If you are not concerned with localization (that is, you don't need your title to dynamically change its name within the Xbox Dashboard based on the current language setting), you can just use the /TESTNAME switch to specify a name for your title. Otherwise, you need to provide a metadata file to specify your title information for each of the languages that you wish to support. Here are the general rules for creating the title information metadata file:

  • All metadata files are UNICODE text files and should start with the standard signature bytes (0xFF and 0xFE).
  • The headings for the metadata files are case sensitive.
  • If a metadata file supports localization, the following rules apply:
    • The metadata file supports multiple languages in a single file.
    • Bracketed tabs separate the available languages in the metadata file. The bracketed tabs contain the languages defined by two-letter codes:
      • EN – English
      • JA – Japanese
      • DE – German
      • FR – French
      • ES – Spanish
      • IT – Italian
      • KO – Korean
      • ZH – Traditional Chinese
      • PT – Brazilian Portuguese
    • A [default] tab may be used as a default language if all of the six supported languages are not specified in the metadata.
    • If the file begins with no bracketed tab, the text that follows is considered the [default] text.

For example:

[default]
TitleName=My Cool Game

[ES]
TitleName=Mi Fresco Juego

[FR]
TitleName=Mon Jeu D'Amusement

In the example above, an Xbox console with its language set to German will display the default game title (in this case, English) because the [DE] tab was not specified.

Unless you want the title of the game to change languages dynamically within the Xbox Dashboard, just follow the Walk-Through in the Dashboard Metadata topic. You don't have to create the titleinfo file unless you want to support multiple Xbox Dashboard language settings.

Switch File

All the switches can be placed in a file that can be specified on the command line by passing the switch file on the command line immediately prefixed with the @ sign (that is, imagebld @c:\temp\nma00540). The following is an example of a switch file:

/in:testexe.exe
/out:testexe.xbe 
/testid:0x00000001

The Visual C++ IDE

Many of the switches listed here can be set from your Xbox Game project within the Visual C++ IDE. Within the IDE, select Project / Settings / Xbox Image. There are two categories of information that you can provide to ImageBld: General and Title Info. See Dashboard Metadata for more information about the fields on the Title Info page.

Setting the Utility Drive Cluster Size

The /UDCLUSTER switch sets the cluster size for the Xbox console's utility drive. If your game title uses the utility drive to store a small number of large files, it will have better performance with a larger cluster size. For example, a 750 MB utility drive with the default 16 KB cluster size has a file allocation table (FAT) around 96 KB in size. With a 64 KB cluster size, the FAT would only be 24 KB. This smaller FAT size increases the likelihood that a page of the FAT will be in the file system cache, thereby preventing an I/O operation from stalling while waiting to map a logical file cluster to a physical disk cluster. (Keep in mind that the maximum cluster size of the FATX file system is 64 KB.)

Displaying XBE Information: The /DUMP Switch

The /DUMP switch produces a wealth of information about the contents of an XBE file, much of which is helpful in determining certification requirements. The /DUMP switch includes the following data:

  • Image header values
  • Certificate information
  • Library versions
  • Thread local storage directory
  • Section headers

The sample output is this section is drawn from the Trees sample application, and was produced using the following command line:

imagebld /DUMP trees.xbe

Image Header Values

The Image Header Values portion of the /DUMP switch's output contains information about the XBE image as a whole. Here is an example of the Image Header Values section:

IMAGE HEADER VALUES
   00010000 base address
       1000 size of headers
     13A1A0 size of image
        178 size of image header
   3C3A2DAA time date stamp Mon Jan 07 15:22:18 2002
   00010178 certificate address
          7 number of sections
   00010348 section headers address
          0 initialization flags
            16K utility drive cluster size
   94875B22 entry point address
   00105584 thread local storage directory address
      10000 size of stack commit
       1000 size of heap commit
     100000 size of heap reserve
   00010C80 original base address
     142000 original size of image
   00000000 original checksum
   3C3A2DA8 original time date stamp Mon Jan 07 15:22:16 2002
   0001057C debug path name address
   000105C2 debug file name address
   00010568 debug Unicode file name address
   EFA1B612 kernel image thunk address
   00000000 non-kernel import directory address
          6 number of library versions
   00010508 library versions address
   00010548 kernel library version address
   00010508 XAPI library version address
   000105CC logo bitmap address
        2B2 logo bitmap size

Debug path name: C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\samples\Xbox\Graphics\Trees
\Debug\trees.exe

The load (base) address for an Xbox executable is always 0x00010000.

Certificate Information

The Certificate section of output from /DUMP contains information about the certificate used to sign the title. The following example shows the Certificate section:

CERTIFICATE
        1D0 size of certificate
   3C3A2DAA time date stamp Mon Jan 07 15:22:18 2002
   FFFF0603 title id
          5 allowed media types
   80000007 game region
   FFFFFFFF game ratings
          0 disk number
          0 version

   Title name: Trees

      LAN key: 54455354544553545445535454455354
Signature key: 54455354544553545445535454455354

Library Versions

The Library Versions section shows you which versions of the Xbox Title Libraries were used to compile the title, as well as whether those libraries were approved or not. Here is a sample of the Library Versions section:

LIBRARY VERSIONS
   XAPILIBD 1.0.4219.1 [debug] [unapproved]
      D3D8D 1.0.4219.1 [debug] [unapproved]
     D3DX8D 1.0.4219.1 [debug] [unapproved]
   XGRAPHCD 1.0.4219.1 [debug] [unapproved]
   XBOXKRNL 1.0.4219.1 [unapproved]
    LIBCMTD 1.0.4219.1 [debug] [unapproved]

Thread Local Storage Directory

This section of the /DUMP switch's output contains information about the title's Thread Local Storage. The following sample shows the Thread Local Storage section:

THREAD LOCAL STORAGE DIRECTORY
   00000000 raw data start address
   00000000 raw data end address
   00145150 TLS index address
   00000000 TLS callbacks address
          C size of zero fill
          0 characteristics

Section Headers

For each XBE section in the title, the /DUMP switch produces a Section Header segment, as shown in the following example:

SECTION HEADER #2  D3D
      52AE0 virtual address
      60B38 virtual size
      43000 file pointer to raw data
      3B8E4 size of raw data
   000104D2 head shared page reference count address
   000104D4 tail shared page reference count address
          7 flags
            Writeable
            Preload
            Executable
